summary: Tested the new Mobile + Desktop banner image option in the Hero Slider end-to-end. Backend: 5/6 pytest cases pass for /api/settings slider_slides shape, legacy slider_images auto-sync, admin guard, whitespace trimming and slider_interval persistence. ONE backend bug found: PUT /api/settings does NOT filter out fully-empty slides (the spec says it should). Frontend: admin /admin/settings panel renders all 5 slider slots each with desktop+mobile sub-fields (URL input, file upload button, clear button, preview), Save Slider button persists slider_slides correctly. E2E verified: set mobile URL on slide 0 -> save -> desktop viewport (1920) loads pexels desktop URL via fallback, mobile viewport (375) loads the example.com mobile URL via . Auto-advance, prev/next buttons, dots-0..4 all functional (clicking next moved active dot from 0 -> 1). slide-level clear-all wipes both desktop+mobile, per-variant clear works, slider-interval-input persists (saved 5, then restored 3). No console errors during the run.

minor.issue: Spec says 'Empty slides (both desktop and mobile blank) are filtered out by the PUT route before saving (after .trim())', but server.py /api/settings PUT handler (lines 770-776) only trims and re-builds slider_slides — it does NOT drop slides whose desktop AND mobile are both empty after trim. Reproduced by PUTting [{desktop:'A',mobile:'B'},{desktop:'C',mobile:''},{desktop:'',mobile:''}]; subsequent GET returns 3 slides (the third being {desktop:'',mobile:''}) instead of 2. Fix: add `.filter(lambda s: s['desktop'] or s['mobile'])` after the trim list-comp. Note: frontend AdminSettings.saveSliderSettings already filters client-side, so the UI flow is unaffected, but any direct API consumer would persist empties.

issues: Mobile preview pane uses a narrower 140px max-width which is cosmetically good, but the Mobile column on viewports between md (768px) and lg ends up wider than the preview — minor visual unevenness; acceptable for this MVP.

action_items: Backend: filter fully-empty slides (both desktop and mobile blank) in PUT /api/settings before saving — single-line fix in server.py around line 774.

(Carry-over) _slugify still strips underscores; DELETE /api/categories cascade; PUT /api/categories non-ObjectId 500; server.py >1100 lines; CORS allow_origins=['*'] together with allow_credentials=True.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py PUT /api/settings does not filter empty slider_slides — spec mismatch, see backend_issues.minor.
HeroSlider.js correctly uses <picture><source media='(max-width:768px)'> — no JS resize listener needed; browser handles viewport selection. Verified empirically at 1920 vs 375. Good implementation.
AdminSettings.js (~420 lines) is approaching the size limit; consider extracting the SlideImageField component to its own file (it's already a separate function but lives in the same module).
AdminSettings.fileInputRefs uses a shared ref-map keyed by `${idx}-${variant}` — works but the refs are created on each render via inline `ref={el => ...}`; harmless but worth noting if perf matters.
HeroSlider FALLBACK_IMAGE is a hardcoded Pexels URL; consider sourcing from env/config so brand can rotate fallback without code change.

Backend: get_settings returns dict directly without a Pydantic response_model, so slider_slides nested objects aren't validated on the GET path (only on PUT via SliderSlide). Acceptable but a typed response would harden the contract.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_slider_slides_mobile.py (new pytest file with 6 tests for slider_slides desktop+mobile)

success_rate.backend: 83% (5/6 pytest cases — the 1 failure is the empty-slide-filter spec gap, code defect not test defect)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin login, 5 slots x desktop+mobile sub-fields present, save-slider persistence, desktop viewport loads desktop URL, mobile viewport 375x800 loads mobile URL via <picture>, prev/next/dots all functional, clear & clear-all & interval inputs all work, no console errors)

test_credentials: admin@mayur.com / MayurAdmin@2024 (verified working — admin-email-input / admin-password-input testids, Sign In button by role).
seed_data_creation: Backend test temporarily PUTs synthetic slides then restores to the original 5 pexels desktop URLs (captured via module-scoped original_slides fixture). Frontend test set mobile URL on slide-0 then cleared it before exiting. No leftover test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All slider testids: slider-slot-{0..4}, slider-slot-{i}-desktop, slider-slot-{i}-mobile, slider-slot-{i}-{variant}-url, slider-slot-{i}-{variant}-upload-btn, slider-slot-{i}-{variant}-file (hidden input), slider-slot-{i}-{variant}-clear (X icon, only when value set), slider-slot-{i}-clear-all (only when at least one of desk/mob set), save-slider-btn, slider-interval-input, hero-slider-settings (panel). HeroSlider testids: hero-slider, hero-slide-{i}, hero-slide-{i}-img, hero-slide-{i}-mobile-source (only when slide has mobile set), hero-slider-prev/next, hero-slider-dot-{i}. The + approach means at 1920px the src/desktop URL loads, and at <=768px the source srcSet/mobile URL loads. Use img.evaluate('el => el.currentSrc') to read which one the browser actually picked (NOT just .src). Auth login: data-testid='admin-email-input' & 'admin-password-input' & button text 'Sign In'.
rca of the issue: Backend PUT /api/settings empty-slide-filter gap: the spec/PRD line says empty slides should be stripped server-side as a safety net. Current code in /app/backend/server.py lines 770-776 only normalizes (trim + dict shape) but never filters. Reproduction: PUT slider_slides=[{desktop:'',mobile:''}] -> GET returns [{desktop:'',mobile:''}] persisted in Mongo. Mitigation: replace `update_data['slider_slides'] = [ {...} for s in update_data['slider_slides'] ]` with `update_data['slider_slides'] = [ {desktop: ds, mobile: ms} for s in update_data['slider_slides'] for ds,ms in [((s.get('desktop') or '').strip(), (s.get('mobile') or '').strip())] if ds or ms ]` — or simpler, do the trim then `[s for s in ... if s['desktop'] or s['mobile']]`.
